text:
CT SCAN OF THE CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
POST-OPERATIVE BASELINE STUDY

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Ms. Margaret Osei-Bonsu is a 47-year-old female with a known diagnosis of high-grade serous carcinoma of bilateral ovaries and fallopian tubes, FIGO stage IIIC, who approximately six weeks ago underwent primary debulking surgery including exploratory laparotomy, total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, infracolic omentectomy, appendectomy, bilateral pelvic and para-aortic lymph node dissection, resection of peritoneal implants, and right hemidiaphragm stripping. Complete gross resection (R0) was documented at time of surgery. This study is obtained to assess post-operative changes, confirm absence of macroscopic residual disease, and establish a baseline prior to initiation of adjuvant platinum-based chemotherapy.

COMPARISON:
Prior CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with IV and oral contrast obtained at time of initial staging, prior to surgery.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of intravenous iohexol contrast at standard dose. Axial images were obtained with reformatted coronal and sagittal reconstructions. Oral contrast was administered. No significant motion artifact. Image quality: diagnostic.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and airways: The lungs are clear bilaterally. No parenchymal nodules, masses, or consolidations are identified. No ground-glass opacities. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level. No endobronchial lesions.

Pleural spaces: No pleural effusion, either right or left. No pleural thickening or pleural-based nodularity. This represents complete resolution compared to trace perihepatic fluid seen at prior staging examination.

Mediastinum and hila: The heart is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. No mediastinal widening. No anterior, middle, or posterior mediastinal masses. Mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes are within normal size limits; no pathologically enlarged nodes identified. Thoracic aorta is normal in caliber.

Chest wall and soft tissues: Visualized soft tissues of the chest wall are unremarkable. No axillary lymphadenopathy.

Upper abdomen (imaged on chest protocol): The lung bases are clear. No subphrenic collections or fluid.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and morphology. The hepatic parenchyma enhances homogeneously without focal hypodense or hypervascular lesions to suggest metastatic implants or parenchymal disease. No perihepatic ascites. Hepatic vasculature is patent. The gallbladder is present and unremarkable without cholelithiasis or wall thickening.

Bile ducts: The intrahepatic and extrahepatic bile ducts are within normal caliber. No biliary dilatation.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesions. No perisplenic fluid.

Pancreas: The pancreas is normal in size and signal intensity throughout the head, neck, body, and tail. No ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic inflammatory changes or masses.

Adrenal glands: Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in appearance without nodules or masses.

Kidneys: Both kidneys are normal in size and enhance symmetrically. No hydronephrosis or hydroureter bilaterally. No renal masses. No nephrolithiasis identified on this non-dedicated study.

Retroperitoneum and mesentery: No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. The previously described right para-aortic lymph node (1.8 cm at staging) and enlarged pelvic nodes are no longer visualized, consistent with successful surgical lymph node dissection. No mesenteric lymph nodes meeting size criteria for pathologic enlargement. No mesenteric masses or soft tissue stranding suspicious for residual peritoneal implants in the small bowel mesentery. The omentum has been surgically removed; the expected post-omentectomy fat attenuation changes are present in the upper and mid-abdomen without focal soft tissue nodularity or thickening to suggest residual omental disease.

Gastrointestinal tract: The stomach is decompressed and unremarkable. Small bowel loops are normal in caliber and wall thickness without obstruction or ileus. The colon is unremarkable. The appendix is absent (appendectomy confirmed). No intra-abdominal free air. No enteric fistulas identified.

Peritoneal cavity — abdomen: No ascites in the upper abdomen. No perihepatic, perisplenic, or paracolic gutter fluid collections. Previously noted peritoneal implants along the right paracolic gutter and small bowel mesentery are not identified on this examination. No residual peritoneal nodularity along the gutters or under the right hemidiaphragm. The right hemidiaphragm, site of prior diaphragmatic stripping for a 1.5 cm implant, shows expected post-procedural thickening and irregularity without focal enhancing nodule or recurrent soft tissue mass.

---

PELVIS:

Post-operative bed: There is expected post-surgical change in the central pelvis following hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy. The uterus and bilateral adnexa are absent. The vaginal cuff is identified and appears unremarkable with mild post-operative soft tissue thickening along the cuff, without a discrete enhancing mass or nodule. Mild pelvic soft tissue stranding is present, consistent with the expected early post-operative inflammatory and fibrotic changes at this approximately six-week time point.

Fluid collections: A small, well-defined, thin-walled hypodense collection is present in the right obturator fossa, measuring approximately 2.1 x 1.8 cm in cross-section (craniocaudal dimension approximately 2.4 cm). This has the characteristic appearance of a lymphocele, with near-water attenuation contents and no internal septations, debris, or peripheral enhancement to suggest superinfection or hemorrhage. This is a known and expected complication of pelvic lymph node dissection. No additional pelvic fluid collections are identified. No pelvic free fluid or ascites.

Pelvic lymph nodes: No pathologically enlarged pelvic lymph nodes are identified. The previously described right external iliac and left obturator lymph nodes are no longer apparent, consistent with lymphadenectomy. Surgical clips are present in the bilateral iliac, obturator, and pelvic sidewall dissection beds.

Peritoneal cavity — pelvis: No peritoneal nodules along the pelvic peritoneum, vesicouterine folds (site of prior implants), or cul-de-sac. No pelvic sidewall soft tissue masses. No sigmoid mesenteric nodularity. The previously identified left pelvic sidewall implants measuring up to 1.4 cm are not visualized.

Urinary bladder: The bladder is partially filled and has a normal wall thickness without mass or mucosal irregularity. A Foley catheter is in situ (appearing as a low-attenuation intraluminal device).

Vascular: Iliac vasculature is patent bilaterally. No vascular injury or thrombosis identified.

Bony structures: Visualized osseous structures of the thorax, lumbar spine, sacrum, and pelvis are intact. No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions. No pathologic fracture. Post-surgical hardware or pelvic fixation devices are absent.

Soft tissues: Midline vertical laparotomy scar is identified. No wound dehiscence, hematoma, or subcutaneous collection along the surgical incision line. Subcutaneous fat is preserved.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Expected post-operative appearance following primary debulking surgery (total abdominal hysterectomy/bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, omentectomy, appendectomy, pelvic and para-aortic lymphadenectomy, peritoneal implant resection, and right hemidiaphragm stripping) for FIGO stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma. No evidence of macroscopic residual or recurrent disease in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis.

2. A small right obturator fossa lymphocele measuring approximately 2.1 cm is identified, representing a common, expected post-lymphadenectomy finding. This is not compressing adjacent structures and does not require intervention at this time.

3. Complete resolution of the prior moderate-volume ascites and bilateral adnexal masses.

4. No pleural effusion, no pulmonary nodules, no new lymphadenopathy.

5. No peritoneal implants, no omental nodularity, no paracolic gutter or pelvic sidewall soft tissue deposits identified.

This study is appropriate for use as a pre-chemotherapy baseline. Findings are concordant with the operative report documenting R0 cytoreduction.

Reporting Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Abdominal Imaging Division]
Electronically signed.
